name: Hierarchical Layout Configuration
description: Optimized force-directed layout for hierarchical knowledge visualization

forceDirectedAlgorithm.description: Force-directed graph layout using modified Fruchterman-Reingold algorithm

centerForce.description: Strength pulling nodes toward center (0-1). Lower = more spread out
centerForce.tuning: Use 0.2-0.4 for hierarchical structures, 0.5-0.8 for clustered networks

repelStrength.description: Force pushing nodes apart. Higher = more separation
repelStrength.tuning: 600-1000 for dense graphs, 300-600 for sparse graphs

linkStrength.description: Strength of connections between linked nodes (0-1)
linkStrength.tuning: 0.3-0.5 for flexible clustering, 0.6-0.9 for tight hierarchies

linkDistance.tuning: 100-200 for compact layouts, 200-400 for expanded views

layering.description: Organize nodes by hierarchy depth from root nodes

clustering.description: Group related nodes using community detection

edgeBundling.description: Bundle edges to reduce visual clutter (may reduce readability)

nodeSizeMultiplier.description: Scale factor for node sizes (1.0 = default)

textFadeMultiplier.description: How quickly text labels fade with zoom (0 = always visible)
textFadeMultiplier.recommendation: Use 0 for always-on labels, 1-2 for zoom-dependent labels

orphanNodes.description: Show nodes with no connections at graph edges with reduced opacity

performanceSettings.description: Performance tuning for large graphs (500+ nodes)

applyingSettings: Adjust sliders to match the values in layoutSettings
Use Filters section to hide orphan nodes if desired

tuningForDifferentGraphs.smallGraphs: (<100 nodes): Increase linkStrength to 0.7, reduce repelStrength to 500
tuningForDifferentGraphs.largeGraphs: (500+ nodes): Increase repelStrength to 1200, reduce scale to 1.0
tuningForDifferentGraphs.deepHierarchies: Increase linkDistance to 250, reduce centerForce to 0.2

troubleshooting.slowPerformance: Reduce maxVisibleNodes, use filters to hide archived content

integrationNotes.dataviewQueries: Use with Dataview to dynamically filter visible nodes
integrationNotes.colorSchemes: Combine with graph-colors-*.json for enhanced visual organization
integrationNotes.filters: Apply graph-filter-*.json to focus on specific subgraphs
